pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates synthetic road network at this exact path
export_result.sh line 17 checks for EXPECTED_FILE at this path

setup_task.sh creates 3 vertical lines (x0, x1, x2) and 2 horizontal lines (y0, y1) forming a 2-column x 1-row grid. Lines to Polygons creates 2 closed polygons. The 6th line (dangle) does not form a closed polygon.
task.json description requires Field Calculator to create 'area_ha' field; verifier.py checks for this field (line 56, case-insensitive match)

Geodesic calculation: Block dimensions are 0.002° lon x 0.002° lat. At latitude 37.701°N, this equals ~176.16m x 222.64m = 39,219 m² = 3.9219 hectares per block. The synthetic data produces blocks within the 3.0-5.0 range.
Geodesic calculation: Same as min_area_ha. The 3.92 ha per block is less than the 5.0 ha maximum, so the bounds are plausible.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic GeoJSON data generated programmatically by setup_task.sh, representing a grid of roads (3 vertical and 2 horizontal lines near San Francisco coordinates: -122.404 to -122.400 longitude, 37.700 to 37.702 latitude). The 'Lines to Polygons' operation will produce 2 closed polygon blocks, each approximately 3.92 hectares (176m x 222m). A 6th line segment (dangle) will not form a closed polygon. The task requires calculating and storing block areas in a field named 'area_ha', with expected values between 3.0 and 5.0 hectares. All metadata claims are verified against the synthetic data structure and geodesic area calculations.
